patterns.description: PEP 649/749: Use deferred annotation evaluation in Python 3.14+. No quotes needed for forward references. Use annotationlib.get_annotations() for introspection.
patterns.code_snippet:
from annotationlib import get_annotations, Format

def process(item: MyClass) -> Result:
    """No string quotes needed for forward refs in 3.14+."""
    ...

# Safe introspection:
anns = get_annotations(process, format=Format.FORWARDREF)

patterns.description: PEP 750: Template string literals (t-strings) for safe interpolation. Use t'...' prefix for sanitization in SQL, HTML, shell contexts.
PEP 734: Use concurrent.interpreters for true multi-core parallelism without GIL contention. InterpreterPoolExecutor for pool-based usage.
patterns.code_snippet:
from concurrent.futures import InterpreterPoolExecutor

def cpu_heavy(data: bytes) -> bytes:
    # Each interpreter has its own GIL
    return process(data)

async def parallel_compute(chunks: list[bytes]) -> list[bytes]:
    with InterpreterPoolExecutor() as pool:
        results = list(pool.map(cpu_heavy, chunks))
    return results

patterns.description: Python 3.14 asyncio: Always use asyncio.run(), never get_event_loop(). Use capture_call_graph() for debugging. 10-20% faster event loop.
patterns.code_snippet:
import asyncio

async def main():
    # Introspection (new in 3.14)
    graph = asyncio.capture_call_graph()
    asyncio.print_call_graph()

    # create_task with kwargs
    task = asyncio.create_task(worker(), name='worker-1')
    await task

# CORRECT: always use asyncio.run()
asyncio.run(main())

# WRONG (removed): asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete()
# WRONG (deprecated): asyncio.iscoroutinefunction() -> use inspect.iscoroutinefunction()

patterns.description: PEP 784: Zstandard compression now in stdlib via compression.zstd. No third-party dependency needed.
patterns.code_snippet:
import compression.zstd

# Compress
compressed = compression.zstd.compress(data)

# Decompress
original = compression.zstd.decompress(compressed)

# Streaming
with compression.zstd.open('data.zst', 'wb') as f:
    f.write(large_data)

patterns.description: PEP 758: except/except* without parentheses in Python 3.14+.
functools.Placeholder sentinel for reserving positional argument slots in partial().
patterns.code_snippet:
from functools import partial, Placeholder

# Reserve first arg, fix second
square = partial(pow, Placeholder, 2)
result = square(5)  # pow(5, 2) == 25

# Skip multiple positions
f = partial(func, Placeholder, 'fixed', Placeholder)
f(a, b)  # func(a, 'fixed', b)

patterns.description: PEP 765: Avoid return/break/continue in finally blocks (SyntaxWarning in 3.14).
patterns.code_snippet:
# CORRECT: finally only for cleanup
try:
    result = compute()
except ValueError:
    result = fallback()
finally:
    cleanup()  # no return/break/continue here
return result

# WRONG (SyntaxWarning):
# try: ...
# finally: return default_value

patterns.description: RFC 3484: extern blocks must be marked 'unsafe' in Rust 2024. Individual items can be 'safe' or 'unsafe'.
RFC 3498: All in-scope lifetime params implicitly captured in RPIT. Use use<..> bounds for explicit control.
Rust 2024: Never type (!) falls back to ! instead of (). Explicitly specify types for generic Ok in Result.
RFC 3513: 'gen' is reserved keyword in Rust 2024. Rename existing gen identifiers to r#gen or another name.
Rust 2024: Box<[T]>.into_iter() yields owned T values, not &T. Use .iter() for references.
RFC 3325: Safety-critical attributes like #[no_mangle] require unsafe() wrapper in Rust 2024.
Rust 2024: Creating references to static mut is a hard error. Use raw pointers or sync primitives.
Rust 2024 prelude adds Future and IntoFuture traits. No manual import needed for async patterns.
TS 5.8 --erasableSyntaxOnly: Replace enums with 'as const' objects and parameter properties with field assignments for Node.js type stripping.
TS 5.8: Use 'with' keyword for import attributes (JSON, etc.) instead of deprecated 'assert'. Required for --module nodenext.
TS 5.7 --rewriteRelativeImportExtensions: Write imports with .ts extensions; compiler rewrites to .js for emit. Works with Deno-style imports.
TS 5.6 Iterator Helpers: Use .map(), .filter(), .take(), .drop(), .toArray() on iterators for functional-style lazy evaluation with IteratorObject type.
TS 5.5 Inferred Type Predicates: .filter(x => x !== undefined) now narrows array types automatically. Use !== undefined instead of !! for correct inference.
TS 5.5 --isolatedDeclarations: Enables parallel .d.ts generation for monorepos. Requires explicit return types on all exports.
TS 5.4 NoInfer<T>: Prevent type widening on 'default' or 'fallback' generic params. Object.groupBy/Map.groupBy available with --lib esnext.
TS 5.5 ECMAScript Set Methods: Use native Set.union(), .intersection(), .difference(), .symmetricDifference(), .isSubsetOf() instead of manual loops.
Python 3.14 uuid.uuid7() — time-ordered UUID for database primary keys. Sortable, better locality than uuid4(). Use uuid7() for PKs, uuid4() for opaque tokens.
patterns.code_snippet:
import uuid

# Time-ordered, sortable — ideal for DB primary keys
pk = uuid.uuid7()

# Random, non-sortable — use for opaque tokens/secrets
token = uuid.uuid4()

# Also available: uuid.uuid6(), uuid.uuid8() for custom formats

patterns.description: Python 3.14 native max-heap functions: heapify_max(), heappush_max(), heappop_max(). No more negate-trick.
patterns.code_snippet:
import heapq

h = [3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6]
heapq.heapify_max(h)        # max-heap in-place
top = heapq.heappop_max(h)  # returns 9 (largest)
heapq.heappush_max(h, 10)   # push onto max-heap

# WRONG (old way): heapq.heappush(h, -x)

patterns.description: Python 3.14 sys.remote_exec(pid, script) — inject debug script into running process without stopping it. PEP 768.
patterns.code_snippet:
import sys

# Inject debugging script into a running Python process
sys.remote_exec(target_pid, '/path/to/debug_dump.py')

# debug_dump.py (runs inside target process):
import traceback, threading
for t in threading.enumerate():
    frame = sys._current_frames().get(t.ident)
    if frame:
        traceback.print_stack(frame)

patterns.description: Python 3.14 incremental GC reduces max pause times. Tail-call interpreter gives 3-5% speedup with Clang 19+. No code changes needed.
patterns.code_snippet:
# Incremental GC — no code changes needed, transparent improvement
# Tail-call interpreter — build CPython with Clang 19+ for 3-5% speedup

# TIP: Don't disable GC unless profiling proves necessity
import gc
gc.isenabled()  # True by default — leave it on

# Stats: gc.get_stats() shows incremental collection metrics

patterns.description: TS 5.8 --libReplacement flag disables built-in lib.d.ts for custom/embedded runtimes. --module node18 is stable for Node 18 LTS.
TS 5.8 preserves computed property names like [Symbol.iterator] in .d.ts files. Granular return branch checks catch per-branch type errors in ternary returns.
TOOL_CALL_ALIGNMENT: When the user message contains a YouTube URL, immediately call youtube_parser. NEVER say 'I cannot browse' — the tool exists and must be called.
patterns.code_snippet:
# User: Summarize this video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XXXXX
# CORRECT agent behaviour:
Thought: The prompt contains a YouTube URL. I must call youtube_parser immediately.
Action: youtube_parser
Action Input: {"url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XXXXX"}
# WRONG (anti-pattern — FORBIDDEN):
# 'As a language model I cannot access YouTube links...'

patterns.description: TOOL_CALL_ALIGNMENT: When the user asks to search the web or provides an http/https URL (non-YouTube), immediately call web_search_mcp or fetch_url. Do not describe intentions — execute.
patterns.code_snippet:
# User: Find information at https://example.com/api-docs
# CORRECT agent behaviour:
Thought: There is an http URL in the prompt. I must fetch it immediately.
Action: web_search_mcp
Action Input: {"query": "https://example.com/api-docs"}
# OR for direct fetch:
Action: fetch_url
Action Input: {"url": "https://example.com/api-docs"}
# WRONG (FORBIDDEN): 'I cannot access external URLs as a language model...'

patterns.description: TOOL_CALL_ALIGNMENT: When the user asks to run, execute, or test code, immediately call sandbox_execute. Do not only show code — execute it and return the real stdout/stderr.
patterns.code_snippet:
# User: Run this Python script and show the output: <code>
# CORRECT agent behaviour:
Thought: User wants code executed. I must call sandbox_execute.
Action: sandbox_execute
Action Input: {"code": "<the code>", "language": "python", "timeout": 30}
# Then observe exit_code, stdout, stderr and report to user.
# WRONG (FORBIDDEN): 'Here is what the code would output...' (without actually running it)
